session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Workflow Markers 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "building multi-step workflows that need state persistence" 7→ - "using erk exec marker commands" 8→ - "implementing objective-to-plan workflows" 9→--- 10→ 11→# Workflow Markers 12→ 13→Markers persist state across workflow steps when a single session needs to pass information between distinct phases. 14→ 15→## Commands 16→ 17→- `erk exec marker create --name <name> --value <value>` - Create/update a marker 18→- `erk exec marker read --name <name>` - Read marker value (empty if not set) 19→ 20→## Use Cases 21→ 22→### Objective Context 23→ 24→When creating a plan from an objective step, markers track the objective for later hooks: 25→ 26→```bash 27→erk exec marker create --name objective-context --value "5503" 28→erk exec marker create --name roadmap-step --value "1B.4" 29→``` 30→
... [40 lines omitted] ...

225→ 226→**To read the saved plan number:** 227→ 228→```bash 229→erk exec marker read --session-id "${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" plan-saved-issue 230→``` 231→ 232→This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session. 233→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

35→If the command succeeds, clear the session marker to allow creating new plans in this session: 36→ 37→```bash 38→erk exec marker delete --session-id "${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" plan-saved-issue 39→``` 40→ 41→Display the command output to the user. The `erk pr dispatch` command handles all validation (plan existence, labels, state). 42→ 43→## Error Cases 44→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

18→ 19→**When these triggers fire and a plan was saved in this session:** 20→ 21→1. Check for `plan-saved-issue.marker` in session scratch: 22→ ```bash 23→ erk exec marker read --session-id <session-id> plan-saved-issue 24→ ``` 25→2. If found (exit code 0), invoke `/local:plan-update <issue-number>` with the marker content 26→3. If not found (exit code 1), ask user for issue number 27→ 28→## Overview 29→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

1→--- 2→title: Session-Based Plan Deduplication 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding duplicate plan prevention" 5→ - "working with exit-plan-mode hook" 6→ - "debugging duplicate plan creation" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "modifying marker deletion behavior in exit-plan-mode hook" 9→ warning: "Reusable markers (plan-saved) must persist; one-time markers (implement-now, objective-context) are consumed. Deleting reusable markers breaks state machines and enables retry loops that create duplicates." 10→ - action: "using session-scoped markers in exec scripts" 11→ warning: "Session markers enable idempotency in command retries. Always write markers AFTER successful operation completion, never before. Use triple-check guard on marker read: file exists AND content is valid AND expected type (numeric for issue numbers)." 12→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 13→audit_result: clean 14→--- 15→ 16→# Session-Based Plan Deduplication 17→ 18→This document explains how erk prevents duplicate plans from being created during retry loops or hook edge cases. 19→ 20→## Problem 21→ 22→When Claude retries a blocked ExitPlanMode call, the same plan could be saved multiple times, creating duplicate GitHub issues. This can happen when: 23→ 24→1. Hook blocks with prompt → user chooses "save" → save succeeds → hook fires again 25→2. Network failures during save → retry creates second issue 26→3. Session interruption → user resumes → re-runs save command 27→ 28→## Two-Layer Defense 29→ 30→Erk uses two complementary mechanisms:
... [78 lines omitted] ...

30→ 31→See `push_and_create_pr()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` — it calls `get_pr_for_branch()` and branches on `PRNotFound` vs existing. 32→ 33→**Session-level:** Plan-save operations use scratch-directory marker files keyed by session ID. Before creating a GitHub issue, the command checks if this session already created one. This prevents the specific failure mode where exit-plan-mode hook retries cause duplicate plans. 34→ 35→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/scratch/session_markers.py, get_existing_saved_issue --> 36→ 37→See `get_existing_saved_issue()` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/scratch/session_markers.py`. 38→ 39→## The Two-Phase Footer Problem
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

``...[truncated 82 chars]...title against current plan title 4. Return existing PR number if found, None otherwise ```

session.assistant:
Plan execution complete. Here's a summary of the changes:
**Renamed `plan-saved-issue` marker to `plan-saved` across the codebase:**
### Python source (3 files) - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/scratch/session_markers.py` — Renamed `create_plan_saved_issue_marker` to `create_plan_saved_pr_marker`, `get_existing_saved_issue` to `get_existing_saved_pr`, marker file from `plan-saved-issue.marker` to `plan-saved.marker`, updated docstrings from "issue" to "PR" - `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_save.py` — Updated imports and call sites - `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/marker.py` — Updated docstring example
### Tests (3 files) - `packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/scratch/test_session_markers.py` — Updated imports, function names, marker filenames, and comments - `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_plan_save.py` — Updated marker filename assertion - `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_marker.py` — Updated all `plan-saved-issue` references to `plan-saved`
### Documentation & commands (5 files) - `docs/learned/planning/workflow-markers.md` — Updated marker name and terminology - `docs/learned/planning/session-deduplication.md` — Updated function references - `docs/learned/planning/pr-submission-patterns.md` — Updated function reference - `.claude/commands/erk/plan-save.md` — Updated marker name - `.claude/commands/erk/pr-dispatch.md` — Updated marker name - `.claude/skills/erk-planning/SKILL.md` — Updated marker name
**Verification:** 78 tests pass, ruff/ty clean, zero remaining `plan-saved-issue` references.
**PR:** https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9183
